---
title: Week 4
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# This chunk is already finished for you, you don't need to modify anything here
library(tidyverse) #this includes many packages and is the main package used in nearly all data wrangling
library(arrow) #this package handles parquet files
library(skimr) #this is particularly helpful when looking at new data or finding NA values
library(waldo) #we use this to create answer keys for these exercises

solution_columns<-read_rds('data_milestones/solutions_week4_check_columns.rds')


#add some extra functions
source('../functions/fxn_add_event_counts.R')
source('data_milestones/fxn_floor_dates.R')


```

## Create and select columns

In this milestone, you'll write some of the code that was written for you in milestone 3.

1.  The events_formatted.parquet file is read in, named "events" in your environment, and filtered for health events. Filter this further so you only have only one or 2 unique health events. The example in the solution is based on 'NAVEL' and 'SEPTIC' events, but choose anything you want.
2.  create a new variable in events named **floordate_month**. The logic to create this variable is: **floor_date(date_event, 'months')**
3.  arrange the events by 3 variables: the animal (id_animal), the event (event), and then the event date (date_event).
4.  Pipe your events to the function fxn_add_event_counts(). This will add columns indicating the number of times the animal has had that event within the data set (event_count_animal) or within their lactation (event_count_lact). If you would like to see the code the creates these click on the function in your environment.
5.  create a new variable the is animal age in days at time of event. Name this variable "age_event"
6.  select all the variables below in the order you see them in the solution_columns table in your environment

```{r}
#| label: recreation-import


events <- read_parquet("../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet") %>%
  filter(event_type == "health", event %in% c("MAST", "METR")) %>%
  mutate(
    floordate_date = floor_date(date_event, "month"),
    #floordate_mo   = month(date_event, label = TRUE, abbr = FALSE),
    age_event      = as.numeric(date_event - date_birth)
  ) %>%
  arrange(id_animal, lact_number, event, date_event) %>%
  fxn_add_event_counts()


# events
# write_csv(events,"excel_excuses.csv")

```

## Check selected columns

```{r}


waldo::compare(colnames(events), colnames(solution_columns))

```

### Step 2 - Disease and Treatments

Create 2 new variables for your events: Disease and Treatment. Use case_when() and whichever columns you would like to create the best description. Then use graphs, tables, or a combination to describe the timing your selected Disease(s), both over time and according to animal age or dim as appropriate. Show if there has been a change in the treatments used over time.

Below are a couple example graphics, but yours can be prettier.

```{r}
#| label: recreate-this
#| message: false


# knitr::include_graphics('images/milestone4_disease_over_time.jpg')
# knitr::include_graphics('images/milestone4_disease_by_phase.jpg')

# ==============================================================================
# Create additional variables of tx_dim_range, year_event, arc_out_dim, arc_out_30?lla
# ==============================================================================

events <- events %>%
  mutate(
    tx_dim_range = case_when(  # categorize dim presentation
      dim_event >= 121 ~ ">120",
      dim_event >= 91 & dim_event <= 120 ~ "91-120",
      dim_event >= 61 & dim_event <= 90  ~ "61-90",
      dim_event >= 31 & dim_event <= 60  ~ "31-60",
      dim_event >= 8  & dim_event <= 30  ~ "8-30",
      dim_event >= 1  & dim_event <= 7   ~ "1-7",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_   # catch missing values
    ),
    year_event = year(date_event),   # extract year from date_event
    arc_out_dim = as.numeric(difftime(date_archived, date_event, units = "days")),
        # did the individual case result in being archived within 30 days of onset
    arc_out_30  = if_else(arc_out_dim >= 1 & arc_out_dim <= 30, 1, 0),
        # archived within 30 days of case onset is assigned a 1, while all others are 0
    locate_qtr = case_when(   # summarize the quarters affected
      nchar(locate_lesion) == 8  ~ "ALL",
      nchar(locate_lesion) == 6  ~ "3Q",
      nchar(locate_lesion) == 4  ~ "2Q",
      nchar(locate_lesion) == 2 ~ locate_lesion, # exactly 2 characters
      TRUE ~ NA_character_       # fallback for missing/other cases
    )
  )
events
```


```{r}
#| label: violin/box_ALL_area
# ==============================================================================
# Coplot of half violin and boxplot for MAST cases - scale = area
# ==============================================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(gghalves)   # needed for half violin plots

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST",
         lact_group != "Heifer") %>%   # filter only MAST events
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year_event), y = dim_event)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, outlier.shape = NA) +   # boxplot parameters
  geom_half_violin(side = "l", fill = "orange", alpha = 0.6) +  # half violin on left
  labs(
    x = "Year of Event",
    y = "DIM @ Event Distribution",
    title = "Distribution of DIM @ MAST Event by Year",
    caption = "Representation by AREA"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 420), 
    breaks = seq(0, 420, by = 30),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 420, by = 30), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  )
```


```{r}
#| label: violin/box_ALL_count
# ==============================================================================
# Coplot of half violin and boxplot for MAST cases - scale = count
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST",
         lact_group != "Heifer", year_event %in% c(2023,2024,2025)) %>%   # filter only MAST events
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year_event), y = dim_event)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, outlier.shape = NA) +   # boxplot
  geom_half_violin(side = "l", fill = "orange", alpha = 0.6, scale = "count") +  # half violin on left
  labs(
    x = "Year of Event",
    y = "DIM @ Event Distribution",
    title = "Distribution of DIM @ MAST Event by Year",
    caption = "Representation by COUNT"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 420), 
    breaks = seq(0, 420, by = 30),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 420, by = 30), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  )
```


```{r}
#| label: violin/box_1st_count
# ==============================================================================
# Coplot of half violin and boxplot for 1st MAST cases - scale = count
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST",
         event_count_animal == 1,
         lact_group != "Heifer") %>%   # filter MAST only & 1st cases
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year_event), y = dim_event)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, outlier.shape = NA) +   # boxplot
  geom_half_violin(side = "l", fill = "orange", alpha = 0.6, scale = "count") +  # half violin on left
  labs(
    x = "Year of Event",
    y = "DIM @ Event Distribution",
    title = "Distribution of DIM @ MAST Event by Year",
    caption = "Representation by 1st cases & COUNT"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 420), 
    breaks = seq(0, 420, by = 30),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 420, by = 30), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  )
```


```{r}
#| label: violin/box_LGRP_1st
# ==============================================================================
# Coplot of half violin and boxplot for 1st MAST cases, 2024 + 2025, by LGRP - scale = count
# ==============================================================================

my_fills <- c(
  "ALL"   = "plum",
  "LACT 1" = "lightblue",
  "LACT 2" = "lightgreen",
  "LACT 3+" = "tomato"
)

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST",
         lact_group != "Heifer",
         event_count_animal == 1,
         year_event %in% c(2024, 2025)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = lact_group, y = dim_event, fill = lact_group)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +   # boxplot parameters
  geom_half_violin(side = "l", scale = "count") +  # half violin on left
  labs(
    x = "LGRP 1st MAST demographics (2024-2025)",
    y = "DIM @ Event Distribution",
    title = "Distribution of DIM @ MAST Event by Lactation Group",
    caption = "Representation by LGRP, 1st cases & COUNT"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 420), 
    breaks = seq(0, 420, by = 30),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 420, by = 30), "")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_fills) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  )
```


```{r}
#| label: stacked_protocols_all
# ==============================================================================
# Stacked bar graph of treatment protocols - ALL treatments
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST", 
         lact_group != "Heifer", 
         year_event %in% c(2024, 2025)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_month, fill = protocols)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +   # adds black border around each stacked cell
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year", 
    y = "Number of Treatments",
    title = "Treatment Protocols by Month/Year",
    caption = "Representation by Time Interval, ALL cases"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 180), 
    breaks = seq(0, 180, by = 20),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 180, by = 20), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BrBG")       # references the 9-class ColorBrewer BrBG palette
```


```{r}
#| label: heatmap_protocols_1st
# ==============================================================================
# Stacked bar graph of treatment protocols - FIRST treatments
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST", 
         lact_group != "Heifer",
         event_count_animal == 1,
         year_event %in% c(2024, 2025)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_month, fill = protocols)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +   # adds black border around each stacked cell
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year", 
    y = "Number of Treatments",
    title = "Treatment Protocols by Month/Year",
    caption = "Representation by Time Interval, FIRST cases"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 180), 
    breaks = seq(0, 180, by = 20),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 180, by = 20), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BrBG")       # references the 9-class ColorBrewer BrBG palette
```


```{r}
#| label: heatmap_pct
# ==============================================================================
# Heat map of removal PROPORTIONS within 30 days of treatment
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST", 
         lact_group != "Heifer",
         event_count_animal == 1,
         year_event %in% c(2022,2023, 2024, 2025)) %>%
  group_by(year_event, protocols) %>%
  summarise(avg_arc_out_30 = mean(arc_out_30, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(year_event) %>% 
  mutate(pct_arc_out_30 = avg_arc_out_30 / sum(avg_arc_out_30, na.rm = TRUE) * 100) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year_event), y = protocols, fill = pct_arc_out_30)) +  # factor() makes discrete axis
  geom_tile(alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct_arc_out_30, 1), "%")), 
            color = "black", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrRd", direction = 1) + # Provides the heat mapping fill
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Treatment Protocols",
    fill = "% of arc_out_30",
    title = "Proportion removed within 30 days of treatment",
    caption = "Filtered: MAST events, non-Heifers, years 2023–2025"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```


```{r}
#| label: heatmap_count
# ==============================================================================
# Heat map of mastitis cases by treatment / year
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST", 
         lact_group != "Heifer",
         event_count_animal == 1,
         year_event %in% c(2023, 2024, 2025)) %>%
  group_by(year_event, protocols) %>%
  summarise(case_count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%   # count cases
  group_by(year_event) %>% 
  mutate(pct_cases = case_count / sum(case_count, na.rm = TRUE) * 100) %>%  # percentage of cases
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(year_event), y = protocols, fill = pct_cases)) +
  geom_tile(alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = case_count),   # show raw counts in each cell
            color = "black", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlOrRd", direction = 1) +   # Provides the heat mapping fill
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Treatment Protocols",
    fill = "Case Count",
    title = "Number of Cases by Year and Protocol",
    caption = "Filtered: MAST events, non-Heifers, 1st cases, years 2023–2025"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```


```{r}
#| label: stacked_qtr_all
# ==============================================================================
# Stacked bar graph of assigned affected quarters
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST", 
         lact_group != "Heifer",
         year_event %in% c(2024, 2025),
         !is.na(locate_qtr)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_month, fill = locate_qtr)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +   # adds black border around each stacked cell
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year", 
    y = "Quarter assignments",
    title = "Affected Quarters by Month/Year",
    caption = "Representation by Time Interval, ALL cases"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 180), 
    breaks = seq(0, 180, by = 20),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 180, by = 20), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BrBG")       # references the 9-class ColorBrewer BrBG palette
```


```{r}
#| label: stacked_qtr_single
# ==============================================================================
# Stacked bar graph of SINGLE assigned affected quarters
# ==============================================================================

events %>%
  filter(event == "MAST", 
         lact_group != "Heifer",
         year_event %in% c(2024, 2025),
         !locate_qtr %in% c("MQ", "3Q", "2Q", "ALL"),
         !is.na(locate_qtr)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = floordate_month, fill = locate_qtr)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +   # adds black border around each stacked cell
  labs(
    x = "Month/Year", 
    y = "Quarter assignments",
    title = "Affected Single Quarters by Month/Year",
    caption = "Representation by Time Interval, Quarter Designations"
  ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 180), 
    breaks = seq(0, 180, by = 20),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 180, by = 20), "")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)  # centers the title
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "BrBG")       # references the 9-class ColorBrewer BrBG palette

# ?geom_half_violin
# ?labs()
events

```

### Extension Options

1)  Improve your description for your chosen events
2)  Choose a new disease
3)  choose to add multiple farms (either your own data or other example herds)
